props.title: Leviathan Runtime Architecture
props.subtitle: Blueprint specification for the universal agent runtime

props.content:
Leviathan is a **fractal agent runtime** built on three invariants: the Finitude Constraint (C1), the Non-Commutation Principle (C2), and the Depth Model. Every module composes through the same config resolver chain, event bus, and validation gate system.

The architecture follows **contract-first development** — schemas and interfaces are defined before implementation, and all inter-module communication uses the canonical `LevEvent` type.

props.title: Core Layer
props.content: Foundational services: config resolution, XDG compliance, event sourcing, process lifecycle. All state flows through the canonical event log.

props.title: Runtime Layer
props.content: Agent execution engine: poly routing, exec transport, model context management. Handles LLM dispatch, tool binding, and agent lifecycle.

props.title: Plugin Layer
props.content: Domain-specific handlers discovered via fractal config scanner. Each plugin declares capabilities in config.yaml and registers poly commands.

props.title: Surface Layer
props.content: Transport adapters: CLI, MCP, HTTP, gRPC. Surfaces are thin wrappers that marshal between the transport protocol and surface-agnostic handlers.

items.title: Request Ingress
items.description: Surface adapter receives request, validates auth, marshals to LevEvent

items.title: Poly Routing
items.description: Config resolver identifies the target handler by command namespace and plugin ownership

items.title: Handler Execution
items.description: Handler processes the event, optionally dispatching sub-agents via exec-transport

items.title: Event Emission
items.description: Results published to event bus, persisted to canonical event log at ~/.local/share/lev/events.jsonl

items.title: Response Egress
items.description: Surface adapter marshals the response back to the originating transport protocol
